quote.text: No man is good enough to govern another man, without that other's consent.
quote.author: Abraham Lincoln, Peoria speech, 16 October 1854

stories.headline: Trump crowns America "Guardian of the Strait," collects a 20% toll for about a day, then trades it for investment pledges
stories.dek: The blockade and the strikes kept going. The one genuinely new thing in Monday's announcement — the toll — didn't survive Tuesday afternoon.

stories.kicker: The Macro Desk · Market Explanation, Not Advice
stories.headline: A cooler-than-expected CPI print collides with a war-risk premium that refuses to leave oil
stories.dek: Tuesday delivered a clean disinflation relief rally — until fresh strikes and a blockade kept crude climbing anyway.

stories.headline: A Chinese probe arrives at Earth's "mini-moon" the same week its origin story falls apart
stories.dek: Tianwen-2 reached the quasi-satellite Kamoʻoalewa just as new evidence undercuts the theory that it's a chip off the Moon itself.

stories.headline: Q2 growth beats forecast at 5.7% — MTI says the risks from the Gulf just got bigger, not smaller
stories.dek: A manufacturing surge carried the quarter as services cooled; a rare "red alert" haze outlook sits oddly against a calm week of actual air quality.

stories.headline: Jurong Lake District tries again, smaller and safer — while two ageing en-blocs go quiet past their own deadlines
stories.dek: A second, more cautious white-site tender follows 2024's rejected mega-bid; Balestier Regency and High Point's results stay unannounced weeks after their tenders closed.

stories.headline: DeepSeek misses its own GA date — and goes shopping for a $71 billion valuation instead
stories.dek: V4 is still labelled "preview" past the mid-July ship promise from No. 42; the real news is a funding round, IPO prep, and Nvidia quietly cutting its Asian buyer list in half.

stories.headline: Meta locks its camera glasses down just as a no-camera pair makes the case for the opposite bet
stories.dek: A tamper-proof kill switch and a "super sensing" prototype on one side; a heads-up display designed to be ignored on the other.

stories.kicker: The Connected Home · Two Philosophies, Continued
stories.headline: Home Assistant and Hue ship real infrastructure this month; Apple's promises still wait on an unreleased iOS
stories.dek: A migrator's hub gets natural-language automations and a real Matter server today; the "biggest HomeKit update in years" is still vapourware from a shipping-reader's chair.

stories.headline: Sony's $650 anniversary flagship loses a sound test to its own $450 sibling
stories.dek: The materials win; the noise-cancelling and sound quality don't — plus the Rainbow Daytona mystery finally explained, and Singapore Airlines' summer network gets bigger.

stories.headline: The reviews for The Odyssey are in; the box-office numbers aren't — yet
stories.dek: Near-unanimous raves, one sharp dissent, and a tracking estimate that's climbed to $132 million — all a day before Singapore cinemas open it.

stories.kicker: The Family Desk · The Numbers at Home
stories.headline: Over half of Singapore's 8-year-olds have already used AI — and the legislative wave keeps targeting a different use case
stories.dek: An A*STAR/NUS study puts a reader's own daughter's age bracket at the centre of the debate, while the global chatbot-safety push keeps aiming at companion bots, not homework helpers.

stories.headline: Fewer Singaporeans are marrying — but the ones who do report being happier than any cohort on record
stories.dek: MSF's first-ever marriage-quality survey lands a genuinely different counter-note to America's "dating recession."

stories.headline: Reconsider your ring's "Zone 2" label — the science behind it is getting messier, not cleaner
stories.dek: A review circulating in exercise-science circles this month questions the easy-pace orthodoxy for anyone training under six hours a week.

stories.headline: ENHYPEN's on-sale opens today — BIGBANG's Singapore date stays dark while its own tour's Goyang leg sells out
stories.dek: One K-pop chase resolves on schedule; the other now looks stranger with every week that passes.

stories.headline: The Twenty Percent That Wasn't
stories.dek: On edicts, mini-budgets and a soft drink nobody asked to have fixed — and the much older lesson that an order is only as strong as the willingness of everyone else to obey it.
